LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUSAN CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF J. STEPHEN GRAZIANI. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (INTERFERENCE WITH ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE) AND 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION (DEFAMATION). PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD BOTH CAUSES OF ACTION WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY. UNCLEAR WHETHER PLAINTIFF ALLEGES INTERFERENCE WITH A CURRENT ECONOMIC RELATIONSHIP, OR PROSPECTIVE ONE, AND WITH WHOM. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE SPECIFICS OF FALSE STATEMENTS, OR PROVIDE ANY DETAILS. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION (HARASSMENT) WHICH IS NOT A RECOGNIZED CAUSE OF ACTION, HOWEVER PLAINTIFF MAY AMEND TO ALLEGE INCIDENTS, IF ANY, THAT DO CONSTITUTE A RECOGNIZED CAUSE OF ACTION. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO PURSUE INJUNCTIVE RELIEF FOR CIVIL HARASSMENT, HE MUST FOLLOW THE PROCEDURES SET FORTH IN CCP SECTION 527.6. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND CONTINUED THE MATTER TO 03/24/11 AT 9:30AM (NOT 9:00AM) TO BE HEARD WITH OTHER MOTIONS AND FOR COURT TO REVIEW OPPOSITION. PLAINTIFF LODGED COURTESY COPY OF OPPOSITION WITH THE COURT. PARTIES TO MEET & CONFER IN AN EFFORT TO RESOLVE ANY ISSUES. COUNSEL MAY CONTACT THE COURT BY TELEPHONE FOR GUIDANCE TO RESOLVE ANY REMAINING ISSUES. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SUSAN CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF J. STEPHEN GRAZIANI.

APPEARANCES: James Attridge, Esq. 552-3088 for pltf & opposing party; David M. Lederman, Esq. of Law Office of David M. Lederman 925/522-8889 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Demurrer overruled as to first cause of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 2nd cause of action (Interference with Economic Advantage) and 3rd cause of action (Defamation). Plaintiff must plead both causes of action with greater specificity. Unclear whether plaintiff alleges interference with a current economic relationship, or prospective one, and with whom. Plaintiff fails to allege specifics of false statements, or provide any details. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 4th cause of action (harassment) which is not a recognized cause of action, however plaintiff may amend to allege incidents, if any, that do constitute a recognized cause of action. If plaintiff wishes to pursue injunctive relief for civil harassment, he must follow the procedures set forth in CCP section 527.6. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT SUSAN CHAN'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 425.16.

APPEARANCES: James Attridge, Esq. 552-3088 for pltf & opposing party; David M. Lederman, Esq. of Law Office of David M. Lederman 925/522-8889 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - denied. Failure to meet initial burden. No showing that subject statements are made about a public figure or in connection with an issue of public interest. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
